title: General Directorate of Customs has issued a letter regarding the "TPS-OIC Certificate of Origin" (Proof of Origin).
bodyHtml:
The General Directorate of Customs has issued a letter regarding the "TPS-OIC Certificate of Origin" (Proof of Origin).

The letter addresses the following points:

Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Oman have not provided the Ministry with seal samples and subsequent inspection addresses. They have not fulfilled the obligations stated in the first paragraph of Article 26 of the TPS-OIC Rules of Origin Agreement.

Pakistan and Malaysia, on the other hand, have fulfilled all legal obligations related to the implementation of the Agreement. However, it has been learned that they do not issue TPS-OIC Certificates of Origin in their own countries and do not accept TPS-OIC Certificates of Origin issued by other countries.

In this context, to prevent the possible grievances of our exporters in the export to countries that do not effectively implement the mentioned Agreement, it is crucial to inform exporters that TPS-OIC Certificates of Origin issued for these countries may be rejected by the receiving countries, leading to the denial of preferential tariffs based on the said document.

To access the relevant letter, click here
